title: Day Two of My Birthday
description: The day before Shimamura's birthday. Adachi arrives early, determined not to forget this year—and announces it to everyone she meets. A playful, warm morning with a touch of destiny.
content:
'Good morning. Tomorrow is Shimamura's birthday.'

'Such courtesy. I am known as Tomorrow-Is-Shimamura's-Birthday.'

Adachi, who had come all the way to pick me up, opened with a rather innovative greeting. Hearing her 'new name' full of reminders, I went, oh right, tomorrow really is my birthday, even reminding myself.

Strangely enough, most people tend to forget my birthday and let it pass by. Even Adachi somehow realizes it late every year. But maybe she's gotten used to that pattern—this time, she's finally aware of it the day before. It made me quietly emotional, seeing how Adachi keeps reflecting her growth in everything she does. Compared to that, things like what year it is or how old I'm turning feel trivial.

'Morning, Adachi-chan. Isn't it a hassle coming over every day? Why don't you just move in with us?'

'Of course, your mom too,' my mother added with a smile as she came out.

With my mom, if Adachi actually said 'okay,' it wouldn't stay a joke. Adachi looked flustered and dodged giving a real answer.

'Um, good morning… tomorrow is Shimamura's birthday.'

Her proactive effort to spread the news was admirable, but she was becoming even weirder.

Still, my mom wasn't the type to be shaken by that.

'Oh really?'

'Could you not sound so genuinely surprised?'

'I'm not joking,' she said, patting my back—though it felt a bit fake.

'You'll eat breakfast before you go, right? Come on.'

My mom firmly grabbed Adachi's wrist and headed for the kitchen. Being dragged along, Adachi kept mumbling 'birthday…' under her breath, which made me laugh but also worry a little.

What if she keeps this up at school? I might end up becoming the most famous birthday girl on campus.

'Ah—so embarrassing,' I muttered, covering my cheeks.

Come to think of it, since Adachi always arrives so early, she sees my just-woke-up face every time. Should I fix that? Or is that what I should really be embarrassed about?

I lightly slapped my cheeks, giggling to myself.

Before I knew it, a deer had appeared beside me, linking arms and looking up at me proudly.

It was so confident that it almost felt like it was saying, standing next to you, I'm more like a horse.

'Hehe. Actually, tomorrow is my birthday too.'

'Congrats.'

'Hohoho.'

Still holding onto it, I headed for the kitchen. Its antlers were in the way.

※

'Alright, let's go.'

'Yeah. Tomorrow is Shimamura's birthday.'

'…………………………………'

Her determination not to forget this year was almost admirable, but if she keeps this up until tomorrow, I might hesitate to go on a date today. Still, having someone look forward to your birthday this much… that's probably a kind of happiness not everyone gets, no matter how much they wish for it.

I put my bag in the basket, and we rode the bike together. Hooking my foot up and straightening my back, my view opened up.

That feeling—being a little closer to the wind and sky—was unexpectedly refreshing.

'But riding double on a bicycle is bad, so please don't imitate us.'

We're pushing through with the excuse that we're delinquents, after all.

With that imaginary disclaimer running, I set off with Adachi—the mobile announcement vehicle declaring Shimamura's birthday.

'By the way, now that I know your birthday too, I can celebrate it from this year on.'

'Huh? Y-yeah… you figured it out?'

'Hohoho.'

Since I hadn't celebrated it at all before, I looked up, thinking I'd make up for it a little.

'October 19th is Adachi's birthday~'

It's about half a year away, so I laughed, thinking I probably won't be repeating it every day like she does.

'S-so that's how it is… though tomorrow is Shimamura's birthday.'

''So that's how it is'?'

'H-hoho, tomorrow is Shimamura—'

That clumsy way she laughs… I can't help but smile fondly at it.

Adachi probably isn't thinking too deeply—she just genuinely wants to celebrate me. And that alone makes my embarrassment rise up to my throat.

But I also think this:

I can't really know for sure,—but if I hadn't been born on April 10th, maybe I wouldn't have any connection to Adachi, who was born on October 19th. But for us to be born on the days we were, our parents' birthdays, and theirs before them, and all the way back… if you trace it far enough, not a single deviation could be allowed since the very beginning of the world.

That's why neither birthday can be missed. And thinking like that, I can almost understand the urge to feel grateful—to everything surrounding us, to the entire history of Earth, even to the distant origins of the universe—as if offering a prayer.

And something that profound, in the end, can be summed up in just one word:

'Destiny, I guess.'

'Shimamura's birthday?'

It's even turned into a question now—and I couldn't help but laugh, wide-mouthed and unrestrained.
